segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered Job.
Could I say a word? I don't want to upset you but who could keep quiet and not respond?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You've certainly encouraged many people and supported those who are weak.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your advice has helped those who are stumbling not to fall, and you have strengthened those whose knees are weak.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But now you're the one suffering and you're upset.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Wasn't it your reverence for God that gave you confidence and your integrity that gave you hope?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Think about it: since when did the innocent die? Since when were good people destroyed?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: From what I've seen it's those who plant evil and sow trouble who reap the same!
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: A breath from God destroys them; a blast of his anger wipes them out.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Lions may roar and growl, but their teeth still break.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Even a lion dies from lack of food, and the lioness' cubs are scattered.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: A word quietly crept up on me; a whisper reached my ear.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Troubling thoughts came to me in nightmares when you fall into a deep sleep.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: I became terrified and trembled; all my bones were shaking.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Then a breath brushed my face and gave me goose-pimples.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Something stopped, but I couldn't see its face. My eyes could only make out a shape. It was totally quiet, and then I heard a voice:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘Can anyone be right before God? Can anyone be pure before their Maker?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If he doesn't even trust his servants, and he says his angels make mistakes,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: how much more does this apply to those who live in these houses made of clay,  whose foundations are based on dust, who fall apart like clothing to a moth?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Alive in the morning, they are dead by evening. They die, unnoticed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are like tent ropes that are pulled up, and they collapse in death. They die without wisdom.’
segments.speaker: Unknown
